title: Wheeled Vehicle Maintenance Services
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 3 — Upgrade existing Saudi vehicle maintenance workshops to defence maintenance standards with OEM training via IPP
executive_summary:
Saudi Arabia operates the largest wheeled military vehicle fleet in the Arabian Peninsula — thousands of vehicles across the Army and Saudi Arabian National Guard (SANG), including the LAV 6.0, M1126 Stryker, VBCI, and various wheeled armoured personnel carriers. Preventive and corrective maintenance for this fleet is performed through a combination of Saudi military workshops, OEM-contracted field teams, and overseas depot repairs — with Saudi content in technical roles below 30% by most estimates.

Node LS-2 targets the most immediate and achievable localisation opportunity in the land systems sector: upgrading Saudi Arabia's existing commercial and light military vehicle maintenance infrastructure to qualify for wheeled military vehicle maintenance. Saudi Arabia already has a substantial commercial automotive and heavy vehicle maintenance industry — the upgrade required is standards, tooling, documentation systems, and OEM qualification, not fundamental capability.

The Saudi military vehicle fleet generates an estimated $300–450 million per year in maintenance expenditure. Phase I targets 50% in-Kingdom Saudi technician content — a shift that would retain $150–225 million annually and create 800–1,200 high-quality technical jobs.

companies.name: General Dynamics Land Systems (GDLS)
companies.rationale: GDLS supplies the LAV 6.0 and has active IPP obligations in Saudi Arabia. Converting IPP to Saudi maintenance capability investment is the natural engagement path.
companies.contact: GDLS VP Saudi Arabia Programmes → IPP Director

companies.name: Nexter (KNDS France)
companies.rationale: Nexter supplies the VBCI to Saudi Arabia and has IPP obligations. Maintenance training and documentation transfer is an accessible first step.
companies.contact: Nexter VP Customer Support, Middle East

companies.name: Oshkosh Defense
companies.rationale: Oshkosh supplies logistics vehicles to Saudi Arabia and has active field service presence. Transition from OEM field teams to Saudi-qualified workshops is the upgrade path.

companies.name: Ricardo Defence
companies.rationale: Ricardo provides independent defence vehicle technical support and maintenance training programmes. Well suited to establish Saudi maintenance standards independent of any single OEM.

companies.name: Rheinmetall
companies.rationale: Rheinmetall has wheeled vehicle maintenance programmes across multiple countries and is actively expanding its Saudi Arabia footprint.

partners.name: SAMI Land Systems / Service Division
partners.current: Some wheeled vehicle maintenance contracts; programme management
partners.upgrade: Establish SAMI-operated vehicle maintenance centres at 3 strategic Saudi locations; OEM qualification from GDLS and Nexter; hire 200 Saudi vehicle technicians over 24 months
partners.timeline: 18 months to first qualified maintenance centre

partners.name: Abdul Latif Jameel / ALJ Automotive
partners.current: Toyota and commercial vehicle maintenance; large Saudi automotive service network
partners.upgrade: Establish defence-grade maintenance division; MIL-SPEC documentation systems; OEM military vehicle qualification
partners.timeline: 24 months to military vehicle maintenance qualification

partners.name: SANG Industrial Services
partners.current: SANG vehicle fleet maintenance operations; significant wheeled vehicle experience
partners.upgrade: Formalise maintenance standards to MIL-SPEC; establish Saudi technician qualification programme; expand to full diagnostic and component repair

partners.name: Technical and Vocational Training Corporation (TVTC)
partners.current: Automotive and heavy vehicle maintenance programmes; national Saudi technician training
partners.upgrade: Military vehicle maintenance specialisation; OEM-certified training curriculum; placement programme with SAMI maintenance centres
